headline: How The Investment Story For Land Securities Group (LSE:LAND) Is Shifting On Mixed Analyst Signals
summary: Analysts have made only modest tweaks to price targets for Land Securities Group recently, with fair value estimates moving in small steps of around 20 GBp to 23 GBp and one large broker holding its target near £6.70. These shifts line up with a mixed research backdrop, where some firms are trimming targets while others, including Goldman Sachs, are comfortable enough to upgrade their rating and keep fair value assumptions close to £7.01 per share. As you read on, you will see how these...

headline: 57% of Americans Say Their Portfolios Are Too Complex to Manage Alone
summary: A Charles Schwab Modern Wealth Survey 2025 finding that 57% of Americans agree modern investment portfolios are more sophisticated and require more professional guidance sits at the center of a broader shift in how households manage retirement money. The figure spans age groups and aligns with measurable changes in product mix, account structures, and the ... 57% of Americans Say Their Portfolios Are Too Complex to Manage Alone

headline: Ceres surges after Goldman Sachs ups target
summary: Ceres Power Holdings PLC (LSE:CWR, OTC:CPWHF) shares surged 23% to 616p on Wednesday, without much concrete news. The only trigger for the strong gain was a report that Goldman Sachs had hiked its share price target to 670p from 530p. The US bank has a 'buy' rating on the solid oxide fuel...

headline: Aidoc Raises $150 Million Series E Led by Goldman Sachs to Scale Clinical AI for Earlier, Safer Diagnoses
summary: Aidoc, a global leader in clinical AI, has raised $150 million in Series E funding led by Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives. The round had participation from General Catalyst, SoftBank Investment Advisors and NVentures (NVIDIA's venture capital arm). The round brings total funding to over $500 million, less than a year after a growth round led by General Catalyst and Square Peg. This underscores the pace of Aidoc's momentum and the accelerating demand for enterprise-scale clinical AI.

headline: Hightouch Raises $150 Million to Reinvent How Marketing Works Using AI
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 29, 2026--Hightouch, the leader in data and AI for marketing, today announced a $150 million Series D financing led by Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives and Bain Capital Ventures, valuing the company at $2.75 billion. Additional investors and strategic partners also participated in the round, including Iconiq Capital, Sapphire Ventures, Amplify Partners, Y-Combinator, and TD7, the venture capital arm of The Trade Desk.
